text.title: user-control-freedom Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for user-control-freedom as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: No undo for frequently performed actions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Users who make frequent mistakes in high-volume workflows lose trust quickly when recovery requires more effort than the original action.

failure-mode.description: Hidden or hard-to-find Cancel buttons in forms and dialogs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Users who cannot find the exit feel trapped; Cancel must be visually prominent and near the primary action button.

failure-mode.description: Confirmation dialog overuse — "Are you sure?" on every action including non-destructive ones.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Confirmation fatigue causes users to dismiss dialogs without reading, defeating their purpose for high-stakes actions.

failure-mode.description: Agent audit based on action names alone may miss context-dependent severity.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: "Delete" in a shopping cart (trivially reversible) is different from "delete account" (high stakes). Always provide action context.

failure-mode.description: Agent cannot detect focus traps in rendered interfaces without accessibility tooling.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always pair agent audit with axe-core or pa11y run. Agent identifies structural absence of controls from descriptions; actual keyboard navigation testing requires browser tooling.

failure-mode.description: Assuming undo/cancel implementation decisions are within agent scope.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Undo/cancel implementation decisions require engineering sign-off; agent audit identifies the need, not the implementation approach. Undo architecture depends on backend patterns that require engineering judgment.

failure-mode.description: Accepting agent recommendation of confirmation dialogs without review.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agent may recommend confirmation dialogs for actions where the better solution is undo (more modern, less disruptive); review recommendations against the undo-vs-confirmation heuristic.

failure-mode.description: Trusting agent verification of keyboard shortcuts without browser testing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Keyboard shortcut verification (Escape, Ctrl+Z) requires live browser testing; agent cannot substitute for Playwright or manual keyboard navigation checks.
